meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement analytics to prove user impact and overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building static clones and start shipping data-driven features. By integrating analytics, you transform a simple student project into a professional-grade case study. You will prove user engagement, retention, and feature impact—the exact metrics hiring managers look for in high-performing junior engineers.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the code compiles, but silence follows. You have built a functional app, yet it sits in a vacuum, invisible to the recruiters who matter. The 'no portfolio' syndrome isn't about a lack of code; it is about a lack of evidence. You are currently a student who writes scripts; you need to become an engineer who ships products.

Today, we break the cycle. We are moving beyond basic UI by embedding real-time analytics into your project. This isn't just about tracking clicks; it is about documenting the 'why' behind your design choices, turning your GitHub repository into a data-backed narrative that commands attention during interviews.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one metric that proves your app solves a problem. This focuses your implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest the top 3 user engagement metrics I should track to demonstrate product-market fit to a potential employer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project README and suggest a North Star metric that demonstrates user value.'

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity Analytics
steps.description: Inject the Antigravity SDK to capture telemetry without bloating your bundle size.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the boilerplate code to initialize Antigravity analytics in a React/Next.js environment, ensuring it captures custom events.

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Flows
steps.description: Tag the specific interaction points where the user achieves value, such as form submissions or feature toggles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook that wraps my main action buttons to send event data to Antigravity with metadata about the user's current session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a custom useAnalytics hook to track button clicks and form submissions.'

steps.title: Visualize Data for the Portfolio
steps.description: Create a dashboard view in your app that displays these metrics, serving as a 'live' demo of your engineering capability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard component that fetches and renders real-time analytics data from Antigravity, styled for a professional portfolio view.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'Generate an AdminDashboard component that displays key engagement metrics.'

steps.title: Document Impact in README
steps.description: Translate your data into a compelling case study. Employers hire based on results, not just lines of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 'Results' section for my README that uses the data collected to prove how my app improved user experience.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Update the README to include a data-driven section explaining the user impact of the project.'

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-value' as a metric; it is the most impressive stat for a junior developer to showcase.
Use environment variables for your analytics keys to show you understand security and production best practices.
Don't track everything; focus on events that show user journey progression to avoid 'vanity metrics' that recruiters ignore.
Create a 'Privacy Policy' snippet in your footer—it shows you understand the legal responsibilities of handling user data.
Include a 'Data Insights' link in your navigation that leads to your analytics dashboard; it acts as a unique selling point for your app.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://analytics-handbook.dev
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, share the link with your personal analytics dashboard visible, and update your LinkedIn with the specific metrics you improved.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a data-driven builder ready to ship. Take this momentum, refine your dashboard, and start applying to roles with a portfolio that speaks for itself. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
